incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the bags. How many bags do you see, and how many items in each bag?

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

params.description: The first slot highlights, then the second slot highlights, then the product slot highlights in sequence.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. First slot shows how many groups. Count the bags. Second slot shows the size of each group. Count the items in one bag. Then skip-count by that number to find the product.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Tiles animate into position: 6 slides into first slot, 5 into second slot, 30 into product slot.

steps.text: Let me show you. There are 6 bags, so 6 is the first factor. Each bag has 5 items, so 5 is the second factor. Now I skip-count by 5s, six times: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. The product is 30. The equation is 6 times 5 equals 30.
